subject: Anthropology
title: Land and Colonialism
description: Learn Land and Colonialism in one minute.

hook.text: Colonizers drew maps and claimed land that people had lived on for millennia. The pen stole what the sword couldn't.

buildup.text: European colonialism imposed private land ownership on societies that held land communally or spiritually.

discovery.text: Terra nullius declared lands empty if not privately owned. Indigenous presence was legally erased with a single concept.

twist.text: Land grabs continue today. Corporations buy vast tracts in Africa and Asia, displacing communities for agribusiness.

climax.text: Property law carries colonial DNA. Understanding land rights means confronting the violence built into ownership itself.

punchline.text: Maps can be weapons when ownership is power.

quiz.question: What was terra nullius?
options: A legal concept declaring lands empty if not privately owned
